title: Ban Ki-moon on Office Hours Podcast
summary: Ban Ki-moon, the 8th Secretary-General of the United Nations (2007–2016), takes a moment to talk with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about Trump’s withdrawal from the Paris climate agreement, how to become UN Secretary-General, and his favorite meals in New England.
body: Ban Ki-moon is the eighth Secretary-General of the United Nations (2007–2016) and former Minister of Foreign Affairs and Trade of South Korea. He earned a Master of Public Administration degree from Harvard's Kennedy School of Government in 1985. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article features Ban Ki-moon, the eighth Secretary-General of the United Nations (2007–2016) and former South Korean Foreign Minister, who holds a Master of Public Administration from Harvard Kennedy School. It positions him within the context of the Belfer Center's 'Office Hours Podcast' and related diplomatic content. The body text primarily lists associated reports, papers, and articles from experts like Bard Harstad, focusing on topics such as tropical forest financing (TFFF, REDD+, SSLB, FLL), outcomes of COP29, and the role of nuclear energy in climate goals. The content serves as a metadata-rich directory linking to deeper analyses on climate policy, diplomacy, and international environmental agreements rather than presenting a narrative interview or analysis of Ban Ki-moon's own statements.
result.main_content_zh: 本文以联合国第八任秘书长（2007-2016年）、韩国前外交通商部长官潘基文为主角，他拥有哈佛大学肯尼迪政府学院的公共管理硕士学位。文章将其置于贝尔弗中心“Office Hours Podcast”及相关外交内容的背景下。正文主要列出了巴德·哈斯塔德等专家的相关报告、论文和文章链接，主题涉及热带森林融资（TFFF、REDD+、SSLB、FLL）、COP29会议成果以及核能在气候目标中的重要性。其内容更像是一个元数据目录，链接到有关气候政策、外交和国际环境协议的深度分析，而非呈现潘基文本人的访谈叙述或观点分析。
